era: 1980-1981. Hardcore Punk. E=10. Seattle, Washington. Independent. THE BEGINNING -- earliest documented Seattle hardcore band. Before Black Flag's Damaged. Before Dischord Records. Before the genre had a name.

theory_nodes.concept: Chronological context for origination E=10: Solger peak 1980-1981. Black Flag Damaged released December 1981 (Solger precedes it by over a year). Dischord Records founded 1980 (same year as Solger peak, D.C. not Seattle). Dead Kennedys Fresh Fruit for Rotting Vegetables September 1980 (same year, different coast). Germs GI 1979. Minor Threat forming 1980. Seattle on the periphery of all of this -- no local hardcore model, no national infrastructure, no touring circuit. Solger playing hardcore in Seattle in 1980 was operating in a near-total vacuum. The energy is not sonic maximalism but origination.
theory_nodes.description: Chronological origination context -- before the genre had a name or infrastructure in Seattle.

theory_nodes.concept: Solger and The Fartz as paired origin: both emerged from same small Seattle punk community 1979-1981. Solger = first, purely independent, underground only. The Fartz = 1981-1983, Capitol Hill, Alternative Tentacles national distribution, Duff McKagan. Together they constitute the founding generation of Seattle hardcore. The engine places them at consecutive degrees (355, 356): two bands, one founding, one city.
theory_nodes.description: Paired origin: Solger + The Fartz = Seattle hardcore founding generation.

theory_nodes.concept: DIY as necessity not choice: in 1980, there was no infrastructure for hardcore in Seattle. No established label, no touring network, no genre charts, no fanzine infrastructure. Playing hardcore meant building every component of the musical infrastructure from scratch. This is the purest expression of the DIY principle -- not 'do it yourself' as aesthetic or ethical preference, but 'do it yourself because there is literally no other option.' E=10 for the origination energy: the foundation of what everything else rests on.
theory_nodes.description: DIY as necessity -- the origination condition in Seattle 1980.

theory_nodes.concept: Engine closing logic: final six degrees organized by energy and foundational significance, not chronology. Blood Brothers (2002-2006) → Solger (1980) → The Fartz (1981-82) → Bikini Kill → Sleater-Kinney → Unwound. The arc: where the tradition arrived → where it began → next generation → where it ended up (Olympia, Kill Rock Stars). The circle closes by returning to origin before completing at destination. Solger at degree 355 = the floor acknowledged at the close.
theory_nodes.description: Engine closing logic -- return to origin before completing at destination.

cross_references.relationship: Previous degree. Most recent band in final six (2002-2006) placed before earliest (1980). The arc from arrival back to origin.
Next degree. Paired origin: the generation immediately following Solger in the same city, same scene. Together = founding generation of Seattle hardcore.

E=10 + E=10 = E=20. Maximum insect emergence: stoneflies (Plecoptera) are one of the most ancient insect orders. The emergence makes the invisible visible, covers the river surface. Parallel to Solger: the underground made visible, the origin emerging into the open.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=10 + E=10 = E=20. The first and the most numerous. Solger = the underground origin, E=10 through priority. Stonefly emergence = maximum insect emergence, the surface of a major river covered by an ancient order. Both: the foundational event made visible.
